comment.body: We can only guess. My thought is the vol around trade has come down significantly and this might add a small uptick to that. Larger partners get UK-like 'deals' and the rest...who knows. Don't think this is massive so don't plan to overplay it. I think he gets tough on sectoral tarrifs when he starts to move on those.

comment.body:
any roll-back of tariffs on CN, there is persistently high trade barriers and a Fed is still unwilling to cut rates simultaneously.
Even if the tariffs would be reduced to 50%, that would still be the largest consumer tax hike since 60s shaving 2-3% off GDP. 
so all in all, market is unlikely to rally until the negative wealth effect forces Fed and Gov to pivot

in the Q&A channel I shared a script for intraday RV, but the mean reversion bands indicator also shows you the real time ROC of RV.
also, the brokerages give you skew metrics, there you can monitor fix strike IV real time.

comment.body: J.K., you asked in the chat last night what put/call ratio people use.  I've tracked several at different times (CPCI, PC) and have settled on CPC.  I believe I saw Helen Meisler reference it as her go-to.  I add a 10-day SMA to it, and just empirically looking back over the last several years, when the 10SMA crosses below 0.85, that's a caution, and when the 10SMA crosses 0.82, the market is ripe for a pullback (in conjunction with other metrics that point in the direction).  As of yesterday's close, the CPC is 0.68 and 10SMA is 0.81.
